arts.ttl: Cameron Carter-Vickers sees pundits wax lyrical over Celtic performance as Hoops lauded for 'fighting spirit'
arts.sum: Cameron Carter-Vickers' vital block to keep Celtic in the lead against Aberdeen left pundits in awe of his talents, as the Hoops make their way down from Pittodrie with a 1-0 win under their belt - with the defender having denied Ester Sokler from scoring a late equaliser. The win puts Celtic seven points clear with a game in hand, and it has seen Carter-Vickers praised for his commitment - with Jonny Hayes stating that the defender's 'fighting spirit' filters through to the whole team and makes them impenetrable.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers champions Celtic diehards hit with Pittodrie punishment as Thelin bigs up mystery man who sickened him
arts.sum: Three points was the reward for the hardy Hoops in the exposed Aberdeen corner and Rodgers made sure to pay tribute

arts.ttl: Celtic team news vs Aberdeen confirmed as fans react to player being 'harshly' dropped
arts.sum: Celtic starting XI : SCHMEICHEL, JOHNSTON, CCV, TRUSTY, VALLE, MCGREGOR, BERNARDO, HATATE, KUHN, MAEDA, KYOGO. With Alex Valle starting ahead of Greg Taylor – as well as Bernardo over Engels – those were two contentious spots for the boss.

arts.ttl: Chris Sutton reacts after Celtic lay down marker with superb Aberdeen triumph
arts.sum: Chris Sutton has delivered his immediate reaction after Celtic made a statement away to Aberdeen to move seven points clear in the Scottish Premiership. Taking to social media platform X, Sutton lauded the contribution of Hatate, Cameron Carter-Vickers and Taylor in what he labelled as an ‘enormous’ win for the defending champions.

arts.ttl: Reo Hatate makes Celtic champions elect already as Aberdeen cut adrift in Pittodrie storm – 5 talking points
arts.sum: A late strike from the Japanese ended Dons' perfect home record in horrendous conditions

arts.ttl: Rangers thrash Kilmarnock as Celtic's win at Aberdeen sees Ibrox side cut gap on second spot
arts.sum: Both goals came during half-time of the Celtic vs Aberdeen game, with news filtering through the stands at Pittodrie - and with both teams drawing at the interval, the as-it-stood table had Rangers breathing down Aberdeen necks, just five points behind with a game in hand. And if that brought stress levels down, then an eight-minute triple salvo in the second half all but wrapped up the win, with Danilo scoring his first goal of the season before returning the favour to Hamza Igamane, who bagged in the blink of an eye to set Ibrox into a frenzy - a rarity this season.

arts.ttl: Celtic strengthen their grip on the title race with victory at Aberdeen
arts.sum: The Hoops are seven points clear.

arts.ttl: Celtic player ratings vs Aberdeen as two stars land 8/10 in statement victory
arts.sum: The opening 45 minutes wasn’t vintage, but Paulo Bernardo may feel unfortunate to have his goal straight from a corner ruled out after Daizen Maeda was adjudged to have fouled Ross Doohan. Reo Hatate would finally achieve that feat in 78 minutes with a brilliant volley from Greg Taylor’s lofted ball through, which would prove enough to settle the scoring and open up a seveb

arts.ttl: Neil Lennon has his say on Celtic disallowed goal vs Aberdeen as referee blows whistle early
arts.sum: “He just needs to stand his ground and nine times out of 10 they’re given as a free-kick against the goalkeeper, whether us old yins think it’s soft or not. Neil Lennon says ref Don Robertson was right to rule out Celtic’s goal

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers explains Celtic changes as boss says 'certain profile of player' needed for Aberdeen title clash
